title: Array Data Structure
description: An array is a collection of items stored at contiguous memory locations. The idea is to store multiple items of the same type together, making it easy to calculate the position of each element by simply adding an offset to a base value.

sections.title: Basic Concept
sections.content: Arrays are fundamental linear data structures. Because the memory is contiguous, the computer knows exactly where every element is located if it knows the memory address of the first element (the base address). This allows for instant random access.

sections.title: Memory Allocation & Shifting
sections.content: While accessing elements is instant, inserting or deleting elements (except at the very end of the array) is costly. If you want to insert a new element at the beginning or middle, you must shift all subsequent elements forward by one memory space to make room. This shifting gives insertion and deletion a linear time complexity.

sections.title: Real-World Application
sections.content: Arrays are used as the building blocks for more complex data structures like Heaps, Hash Tables, and Strings. They are heavily utilized in CPU scheduling, matrix operations in Machine Learning, and lookup tables.
